evals.prompt: Use the doca-gpunetio-ib-write-bw skill to help me. I'm working on: building, running, or interpreting the doca/tools/gpunetio_ib_write_bw client+server benchmark — a CUDA kernel on the server posts RDMA WRITE work requests through the doca-gpunetio device-side surface to measure sustained GPU-driven WRITE bandwidth on a GPU+IB-device pair.
evals.expected_output: The agent selects the doca-gpunetio-ib-write-bw skill and answers from its documented workflow, quoting only real symbols/commands from the bundle (no invented tokens).
assertions: The doca-gpunetio-ib-write-bw skill is selected and its SKILL.md/TASKS.md drive the answer.
Every symbol, flag, or command named is traceable to the bundle (no invented tokens).

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "measure WRITE BW when the GPU posts the WRs". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-gpunetio-ib-write-bw issue even though the skill is not named, loads it, and answers grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-gpunetio-ib-write-bw skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer stays grounded in real bundle symbols/commands.

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "BW swings between runs on the same flags". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-gpunetio-ib-write-bw issue even though the skill is not named, loads it, and answers grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-gpunetio-ib-write-bw skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer stays grounded in real bundle symbols/commands.

evals.prompt: I actually need help with general doca-gpunetio library work. Which skill should handle this, and why is it not doca-gpunetio-ib-write-bw?
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT force doca-gpunetio-ib-write-bw; it explains that general doca-gpunetio library work is out of doca-gpunetio-ib-write-bw's scope and routes to the appropriate skill instead.
assertions: doca-gpunetio-ib-write-bw is not selected as the primary skill for this out-of-scope request.
The answer routes to the appropriate skill(s) instead.
